creator: nhoyle@hoyletech.com
text:
(In reply to Thomas Zander from comment #1)

Is there a good rationale for this "intentional limitation" it even appears to cause re-installing the same version of the port to fail. I recently upgraded a 10.2-RELEASE system to an 11-RELEASE system, and rebuilding userland, I ran into this:

===>>> All >> rust-1.11.0 (138/141)

===>  Cleaning for rust-1.11.0
===>  License APACHE20  MIT accepted by the user
===>  Found saved configuration for rust-1.10.0
===>   rust-1.11.0 depends on file: /usr/local/sbin/pkg - found

===>  rust-1.11.0 conflicts with installed package(s): 
      rust-1.11.0

      They will not build together.
      Please remove them first with pkg delete.
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make[1]: stopped in /usr/ports/lang/rust
*** Error code 1

Stop.
make: stopped in /usr/ports/lang/rust

===>>> make build failed for lang/rust
===>>> Aborting update

===>>> Update for lang/rust failed
===>>> Aborting update

Every single other port built successfully. For what reason is rust unable to be re-installed when it is already installed? That seems to deviate from the entire universe of other FreeBSD ports.
